name: ListboxRoot
description:
Groups all parts of the listbox.
Doesn't render its own HTML element.

name.description: Identifies the field when a form is submitted.

defaultValue.description:
The uncontrolled value of the listbox when it's initially rendered.

To render a controlled listbox, use the `value` prop instead.

value.description: The value of the listbox. Use when controlled.

onValueChange.type: ((value: Value[] | Value | any | null, eventDetails: Listbox.Root.ChangeEventDetails) => void)
onValueChange.description: Event handler called when the value of the listbox changes.
onValueChange.detailedType:
| ((
    value: Value[] | Value | any | null,
    eventDetails: Listbox.Root.ChangeEventDetails,
  ) => void)
| undefined

highlightItemOnHover.description: Whether moving the pointer over items should highlight them.

isItemEqualToValue.description:
Custom comparison logic used to determine if a listbox item value matches the current selected value.
Defaults to `Object.is` comparison.

itemToStringLabel.description: Converts an object value to a string label for display.

itemToStringValue.description: Converts an object value to a string representation for form submission.

loading.description: Whether items are currently being loaded.

loopFocus.description: Whether keyboard navigation loops back to the first/last item.

multiple.description: Whether multiple items can be selected.

onItemsReorder.description: Event handler called when items are reordered via drag-and-drop or keyboard.

onLoadMore.description: Event handler called when more items should be loaded.

disabled.description: Whether the component should ignore user interaction.

readOnly.description: Whether the user should be unable to change the selected value.

required.description: Whether the user must choose a value before submitting a form.

orientation.description: The orientation of the listbox for keyboard navigation.

inputRef.description: A ref to access the hidden input element.
